text: @PeterDiamandis AI probably exceeds the sum of all human intelligence in 4 or 5 years
summary: @PeterDiamandis AI probably exceeds the sum of all human intelligence in 4 or 5 years

parent.text: Abundance isn’t just more compute and more energy. It’s more minds. More people free to invent, create, and solve. The ceiling on human genius has always been the size of the human population, and their access to tools and resources. As humanity moves into space we shatter all
parent.name: Peter H. Diamandis, MD
parent.embed_html:
Abundance isn’t just more compute and more energy. It’s more minds. More people free to invent, create, and solve. The ceiling on human genius has always been the size of the human population, and their access to tools and resources. As humanity moves into space we shatter all…

— Peter H. Diamandis, MD (@PeterDiamandis) June 21, 2026

ai_summary: In response to Peter Diamandis's point that human potential is limited by population size and resource access, Musk argues that AI will fundamentally reshape this constraint by exceeding cumulative human intelligence within 4-5 years. This directly addresses a critical business assumption for AI-driven companies (xAI, Tesla autonomy, etc.): that artificial intelligence will become the primary driver of innovation and problem-solving, making human capital abundance less of a bottleneck than AI capability and compute infrastructure.
embed_html:
AI probably exceeds the sum of all human intelligence in 4 or 5 years

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 21, 2026
